frameworks.name: attractor network dynamics
frameworks.one_line: neural population dynamics organized as attractors mediate perceptual choice

frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: decision arises from integrating pulsatile auditory evidence over time

frameworks.name: unsupervised deep learning of neural dynamics
frameworks.one_line: data-driven discovery of low-dimensional decision-related trajectories

core_question: Do attractor-like dynamics in frontal cortex and striatum underlie perceptual decision-making, and when does commitment occur?
key_assumptions: decision behavior is mediated by attractor-network dynamics
trial-by-trial population trajectories can be recovered from simultaneous large-scale recordings
commitment corresponds to a discrete change in dynamical regime
manipulations: pulsatile auditory evidence accumulated by rats
alignment of behavior to neurally-inferred commitment time (nTc)
key_findings: population trajectories evolve along two sequential regimes
first regime is dominated by sensory inputs; second by autonomous dynamics
the two regimes flow along largely orthogonal neural modes
evidence before nTc affects choice while evidence after nTc does not
interpretations: the transition between regimes marks the moment of decision commitment
a simplified coupled dynamics-and-mode model permits single-trial inference of commitment
connecting_signals: unsupervised deep learning of neural dynamics
